description_long: This bungalow consists of two distinct buildings set on a shared rectangular base, separated by a paved area. The main building features a steeply pitched gable roof with a prominent triangular glass facade, large floor-to-ceiling windows, and a modern, geometric design. The secondary building is a two-story structure with a hip roof, horizontal window bands, and a more utilitarian appearance, likely serving as an annex or guest house. Both buildings have reddish-brown tiled roofs and light-colored walls, with blue-tinted windows providing a contemporary aesthetic. The paved area between the buildings suggests a shared courtyard or driveway, suitable for urban or suburban environments. The overall design blends modern and traditional bungalow elements, emphasizing open glass surfaces and functional outdoor space.
description: A modern bungalow complex with two buildings: one with a steep gable roof and glass facade, the other a two-story annex with a hip roof and horizontal windows, set on a shared paved base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main building's large triangular glass facade and the secondary building's hip roof in the background.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wide glass facade of the main building and the full length of the two-story annex, separated by a paved courtyard.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the two-story annex and the rear elevation of the main building, both with visible rooflines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the annex's hip roof and the main building's glass facade from a different angle, emphasizing the separation between the two structures.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with large front glass facade and horizontal window bands on annex
